question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that when isEnabled() returns false the AbstractStreamFileParser.shouldParse() skips hash-chain verification entirely, allowing a tampered stream file with a broken previousHash to be silently accepted if parsing is later re-enabled without re-checking the gap, leading to corrupted Hashgraph history persisted to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition by causing repeated enable/disable transitions of the parser (e.g., via environment variable injection or config reload) so that a tampered record file is submitted during the window where isEnabled() transitions from false to true, bypassing the hash-chain check in shouldParse() and inserting falsified Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file whose reported StreamType does not match the expected getStreamType() value, causing the parser to skip the chained hash verification (since StreamType.BALANCE returns isChained()=false) and accept a tampered file as if it were a non-chained balance file, corrupting the Hashgraph record history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStreamType() is used as a metric tag in AbstractStreamFileParser to fingerprint which stream types are active, then time a tampered file injection to coincide with a stream type switch (RECORD to BLOCK cutover), causing the hash chain to be evaluated against the wrong previous file and accepting a forged previousHash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.queueCapacity (default 10) by flooding the BatchStreamFileNotifier queue with crafted stream files, causing legitimate files to be dropped when the blocking queue is full, creating gaps in the Hashgraph history that allow tampered replacement files to be inserted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.maxFiles threshold to force the parser to batch multiple stream files together, where a tampered file is bundled with legitimate files, causing the hash chain check to only verify the first file in the batch and silently accept the tampered file's corrupted Hashgraph data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the BatchProperties.maxItems (default 60,000) boundary by injecting a stream file with exactly maxItems-1 legitimate items followed by one tampered item, causing the batch flush to commit the tampered transaction into the Hashgraph history database before integrity checks complete?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.window (default 5 minutes) lag detection mechanism by artificially delaying stream file delivery so the parser believes it is behind, triggering aggressive batching that groups tampered and legitimate files together and bypasses per-file hash chain validation in shouldParse()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.flushInterval (default 2 seconds) timing by injecting a tampered stream file just before a flush event, ensuring the tampered file is committed to the database as part of a batch flush before any out-of-band integrity check can detect the hash mismatch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getFrequency() polling interval (default 20ms for RECORD) by timing a tampered stream file injection to land precisely between two polling cycles, causing the parser to process the tampered file as the next legitimate file without detecting the hash chain break?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getProcessingTimeout() (default 10 seconds) health check window by causing a deliberate parse delay that exceeds the timeout, making the health indicator report UNKNOWN status, masking the fact that a tampered stream file caused a HashMismatchException and halted legitimate Hashgraph history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where getProcessingTimeout() is set to a very small value (minimum 5 seconds per @DurationMin), causing the StreamFileHealthIndicator to report DOWN status prematurely, triggering an operator to manually reset the parser state (last file pointer) and allowing a tampered file to be re-injected as the new chain head?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when isEnabled() returns false, the AbstractStreamFileParser.last AtomicReference is NOT cleared, so a previously cached last-file hash remains stale, and when parsing is re-enabled the stale hash is used to validate the next file, allowing a tampered file with a hash matching the stale cached value to pass chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.queueCapacity blocking behavior (queue.put() blocks when full) to cause a deadlock in the BatchStreamFileNotifier thread, halting all stream file processing and creating a window where the Hashgraph history stops being updated, enabling a history manipulation attack during the outage?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStreamType() determines which StreamFileRepository.findLatest() is queried for the previous hash, by injecting a stream file that causes the wrong repository to be consulted, returning a null or mismatched previous hash that passes the isChained() check and allows tampered Hashgraph history to be persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileHealthIndicator's reliance on isEnabled() to return parsingDisabledHealth (Status.UNKNOWN) when parsing is disabled, using this as a side-channel to confirm that a targeted DoS attack on the parser succeeded, then timing a tampered file injection during the disabled window to corrupt Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties window comparison (lag <= properties.getWindow().toNanos()) by crafting stream files with future consensusEnd timestamps, making the parser believe it is caught up and triggering immediate flush of a batch containing tampered Hashgraph history records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the minimum frequency constraint (@DurationMin(millis = 10L)) to cause a high-frequency polling storm that exhausts the stream file provider, causing legitimate files to be skipped and creating gaps in the Hashgraph history chain that can be filled with tampered files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchProperties has no upper bound on maxItems (only @Min(1)), allowing an attacker who can influence configuration to set maxItems to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the parser to accumulate an unbounded number of stream files in memory before flushing, enabling a memory exhaustion attack that corrupts the in-flight Hashgraph history batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.maxFiles having no upper bound (only @Min(1)) to cause the parser to batch an arbitrarily large number of stream files, where a tampered file deep in the batch is never individually hash-chain-verified because shouldParse() only checks the immediately previous file, allowing tampered Hashgraph history to slip through?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled(false) followed by setEnabled(true) does not trigger a re-validation of the last processed stream file against the database, allowing an attacker to inject a tampered file during the disabled window that becomes the new chain head when parsing resumes, corrupting all subsequent Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamType.BLOCK type (which has no signature files per StreamTypeTest) to inject unsigned tampered block files that bypass the signature verification step, since getStreamType() returning BLOCK causes the downloader to skip signature consensus checks, allowing forged Hashgraph history to be ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier's use of getBatch().getQueueCapacity() to size the ArrayBlockingQueue, by causing the queue to fill up with crafted files that have artificially high item counts, triggering maxItems-based flush of a batch that includes a tampered stream file before the hash chain can be verified against the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency-based queue polling (queue.poll(frequency, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)) in BatchStreamFileNotifier to cause a null streamFile to be passed to the handle() method during a polling timeout, and if the handle() method does not properly guard against null, cause a NullPointerException that resets the parser state and allows a tampered file to be processed as the first file in a new chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMessageLookupEntityListener's isEnabled() check (which calls parserProperties.isEnabled()) to infer the parser state via observable side effects (e.g., absence of topic message lookup records), using this as a timing oracle to determine when to inject a tampered stream file that will be processed without a prior chain anchor?
